title: AI Policy, Compliance & Governance Tools
description: Tools, frameworks, and reference documents for navigating AI regulation, compliance, audit, and governance — across EU AI Act, GDPR, and global frameworks.

tools.name: EU AI Act — Regulation 2024/1689
tools.blurb: The official text of the EU AI Act. Phased applicability through 2027.
tools.our_take: Read in full at least once. The recitals (preamble) clarify intent for ambiguous articles.

tools.name: AI Act Compliance Tracker
tools.blurb: Independent tracker for EU AI Act implementation deadlines, draft templates, and Commission guidance.
tools.our_take: Lower-friction than reading EUR-Lex directly. Update cadence is fast.

tools.name: EDPB Guidance on AI Models
tools.blurb: European Data Protection Board's interpretation of GDPR as applied to AI model training and deployment.
tools.our_take: Critical for anyone training models on EU-resident data. The guidance is firm even where the law is ambiguous.

tools.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
tools.blurb: US federal framework for AI risk management — voluntary but increasingly referenced in procurement.
tools.our_take: Less prescriptive than EU AI Act; more guidance. Useful as a structuring framework even outside US-federal contexts.

tools.name: ISO/IEC 42001
tools.blurb: International standard for AI management systems — auditable, certifiable.
tools.our_take: Worth pursuing if you sell to enterprises or government. The certification is becoming a procurement signal.

tools.name: MITRE ATLAS
tools.blurb: Threat-landscape framework for AI/ML systems — tactics, techniques, case studies.
tools.our_take: Use as the reference taxonomy for adversarial threat modeling. Map your incident response runbooks to ATLAS techniques.

tools.name: OWASP LLM Top 10
tools.blurb: Categorized risks for LLM applications: prompt injection, output handling, training data poisoning, etc.
tools.our_take: The most actionable security checklist for LLM apps. Should anchor any application-tier security review.

tools.name: OECD AI Principles
tools.blurb: International norm-setting framework for AI governance. Adopted by 47+ countries.
tools.our_take: Less actionable than NIST or EU AI Act, but useful for cross-jurisdictional alignment.

tools.name: Drata
tools.blurb: Compliance automation platform — SOC 2, ISO 27001, ISO 42001 evidence collection.
tools.our_take: Pricey but saves substantial audit prep time. The 42001 module is recent.

tools.name: Vanta
tools.blurb: Compliance and security posture automation. Comparable to Drata.
tools.our_take: Pick based on integrations. Both vendors are mature; differentiation is mostly UI and pricing.

tools.name: Credo AI
tools.blurb: AI governance platform — model registry, risk assessment, compliance reporting.
tools.our_take: Most AI-specific of the governance platforms. Worth evaluating if you have multiple regulated AI systems.

tools.name: Model Cards (Mitchell et al.)
tools.blurb: Original model-card template — purpose, training, evaluation, ethical considerations.
tools.our_take: The de facto standard. Customize per organization but follow the structure.

tools.name: Datasheets for Datasets (Gebru et al.)
tools.blurb: Datasheet template for ML datasets — provenance, composition, recommended uses.
tools.our_take: Companion to model cards. Required for credible disclosure under EU AI Act and most enterprise procurement.

tools.name: Hugging Face Model Card guide
tools.blurb: HF's documentation template, more detailed than Mitchell et al.
tools.our_take: Use as the practical template; it integrates with HF model hosting if that's your distribution channel.

tools.name: Microsoft Presidio
tools.blurb: PII detection and anonymization for text data.
tools.our_take: Use upstream of model training and inference for GDPR/CCPA compliance posture.

tools.name: OpenDP
tools.our_take: Mathematically rigorous; production deployment is non-trivial. Worth it for high-sensitivity data.
